name: Emergency Contacts
body: EMERGENCY: Police / Fire / Ambulance — 911. {{org}} office (Mon-Fri 8:30am-5pm): {{office_phone}}. After-hours emergency: {{afterhours_phone}}. Gas smell? Enbridge Emergency: 1-866-763-5427 — leave the building first.

name: Fire Watch — Active
body: FIRE WATCH in effect at {{building}}. The fire alarm system is temporarily offline; trained staff are patrolling. If you see smoke or fire, leave immediately and call 911. Do not use elevators.

name: Fire Evacuation Plan
body: If the fire alarm sounds: leave by the nearest exit stairwell, do NOT use elevators, and assemble at the muster point: {{muster_point}}. Do not re-enter until cleared by Fire Services. Residents needing assistance to evacuate, contact the office to be added to our list.

name: Fire Safety Equipment Testing
body: Scheduled fire-alarm and equipment testing at {{building}} on {{date}}, {{time}}. You may hear alarms during this window — this is a TEST. No action needed unless staff advise otherwise.

name: Extreme Heat Alert
body: HEAT WARNING. Stay hydrated, avoid the sun midday, and check on neighbours. Nearest cooling location: {{cooling_location}}. If you feel dizzy, nauseous or have a rapid heartbeat, call 911.

name: Extreme Cold Alert
body: EXTREME COLD WARNING. Dress in layers and limit time outdoors. Nearest warming station: {{warming_location}}. If your unit has no heat, report it to {{office_phone}} immediately.

name: Gas Leak — Evacuate
body: GAS LEAK: Leave the building NOW. Do not use light switches, elevators, or phones inside. Once outside, call Enbridge Emergency 1-866-763-5427 and 911. Assemble at {{muster_point}}.

name: Building Security Alert
body: SECURITY NOTICE at {{building}}: For your safety, keep unit and entry doors locked and do not let unknown persons in. Report concerns to {{office_phone}} or call 911 in an emergency.

name: How to Submit a Work Order
body: Need a repair? Submit a work order any of these ways: call the building line {{office_phone}}, email {{office_email}}, or visit the office during business hours. Please describe the issue and your unit number.

name: Maintenance Service Standards
body: Our repair response targets: Emergency (health/safety) 24-48 hrs; Priority repairs within {{priority_days}} days; Routine maintenance as scheduled. Timing can be affected by parts, weather, and workload. Charges may apply for damage from misuse or vandalism.

name: On-Call / After-Hours Service
body: For after-hours emergencies (no heat, flood, no power, lockout) call {{afterhours_phone}}. On-call hours: 5:00pm-7:00am, weekends and holidays. Non-emergencies will be handled the next business day.

name: Planned Service Disruption
body: PLANNED service disruption at {{building}}: {{service}} will be unavailable on {{date}} from {{time}}. Please plan ahead. We apologize for the inconvenience — questions: {{office_phone}}.

name: Unplanned Service Disruption
body: We are aware of an unplanned {{service}} disruption at {{building}} and crews are responding. Updates to follow. Thank you for your patience — {{office_phone}}.

name: Water Shut-Off
body: WATER SHUT-OFF at {{building}} on {{date}}, {{time}}. Please store water in advance for drinking and cooking. Water will be restored as soon as work is complete.

name: Electrical Shut-Down
body: POWER SHUT-DOWN at {{building}} on {{date}}, {{time}} for {{reason}}. Elevators and hallway lighting may be affected. Please prepare accordingly.

name: Elevator Out of Service
body: The elevator at {{building}} is out of service. Repairs are underway and we expect it back by {{eta}}. Residents needing assistance, please call {{office_phone}}.

name: Common Repairs — What Staff Check
body: Common repair issues our staff fix: P-trap leaks, fan-coil units, drywall damage, and faucet/toilet repairs. Submit a work order and we'll schedule the right trade.

name: Preventative Maintenance Visit
body: Scheduled preventative maintenance in your unit at {{building}} on {{date}}. Staff will check smoke detectors, plumbing and HVAC. Please ensure access to the kitchen sink and fan-coil area.

name: Pest Control & Treatment
body: Pest treatment is scheduled for {{building}} / unit {{unit}} on {{date}}. Preparation instructions will be provided — please follow them so treatment is effective. Report any pest activity to {{office_phone}}.

name: Bedbug Treatment Prep
body: Bedbug treatment for unit {{unit}} on {{date}}. Please launder and bag bedding/clothing, pull furniture from walls, and clear closets. Do NOT discard furniture without staff guidance. Full prep sheet attached.

name: Waste Diversion — Know Before You Throw
body: Help keep {{building}} clean: Garbage, Recycling (Blue Bin) and Organics (Green Bin) each go in their own chute/room. When in doubt, check the signage. Thank you for sorting right!

name: Bulk Item Pickup
body: BULK ITEM pickup at {{building}} is {{date}}. Bring large items (furniture, appliances) to {{location}} the night before. Do not block hallways or exits. Electronics and hazardous waste must NOT go in bulk pickup.

name: Rent Payment Reminder
body: Friendly reminder: rent is due on the 1st of each month. Pay by pre-authorized debit, online, or at the office. Questions about your account? Call {{office_phone}}.

name: Rent Arrears — We Can Help
body: Behind on rent? Please talk to us early — we have payment plan options and can connect you to financial supports. Contact your Housing Worker at {{office_phone}} before a notice is issued.

name: Payment Plans
body: If you can't pay your full rent on time, a payment plan may be available. Contact your Housing Worker at {{office_phone}} to set one up and avoid further action.

name: How Your Rent (RGI) Is Calculated
body: Rent-Geared-to-Income (RGI) is based on your household's income. Generally, you pay about 30% of monthly household income toward rent. Your rent is reviewed annually — keep your income information up to date.

name: RGI — Reporting Requirements
body: RGI tenants must: file income tax every year, report income changes between annual reviews, declare all assets, and pursue income you're entitled to. Failing to report can affect your subsidy. Questions: {{office_phone}}.

name: Annual Review — Documents Required
body: For your annual RGI review, provide for everyone 18+ in your home: Notice of Assessment (NOA), CRA Proof of Income, ID/status documents, recent Ontario Works/ODSP statement, proof of full-time school enrolment, and verification of any new assets. Submit by the date in your review letter.

name: Over-Housed Notice
body: Our records show your unit may be larger than your household size permits (over-housed). You may be asked to transfer to a right-sized unit. Your Housing Worker will contact you — questions: {{office_phone}}.

name: Annual Market Rent Increase
body: Notice: market rent will increase by {{percent}}% effective {{date}}. Your updated rent amount is shown on your notice. Questions about your account? Call {{office_phone}}.

name: Free Tax Clinic
body: TAX SEASON: File your taxes for FREE at the {{org}} Tax Clinic. Filing taxes keeps your benefits and RGI subsidy current. Dates & booking: {{tax_clinic_info}}.

name: How to Access Your NOA
body: Need your Notice of Assessment (NOA) for your rent review? Get it from CRA My Account online, the CRA app, or by calling 1-800-959-8281. Staff can help — ask at the office.

name: Financial Literacy Supports
body: {{org}} offers free financial coaching, budgeting help, and benefits navigation. Build your financial confidence — ask staff or call {{office_phone}} to connect.

name: Tenant Obligations
body: As a tenant you agree to: pay rent in full and on time, keep your home reasonably clean, repair damage you or your guests cause, be reasonably quiet, follow building rules and by-laws (including occupancy limits), and honour your lease.

name: Pet Policy
body: Pets are welcome if they follow City of Toronto animal by-laws and don't damage property or disturb neighbours. Stoop & scoop, keep pets leashed in common areas, and no smoking in stairwells. Aggressive or nuisance animals may not be permitted.

name: Reasons for Eviction
body: Tenancies can be ended for: non-payment or persistent late rent; misrepresenting income; no longer qualifying for subsidy (e.g., owning a home); not submitting income verification; damage; interfering with others' reasonable enjoyment; overcrowding; illegal acts; or impairing others' safety. Talk to us early to avoid this.

name: Preventing Eviction
body: If you've received a notice, act fast — most issues can be resolved. Pay arrears or correct the issue before the date on the notice, and contact your Housing Worker at {{office_phone}}. Help is available.

name: How to Avoid Getting Notices
body: Avoid notices: pay rent on time, keep your unit clean and undamaged, sort your waste, keep noise down, and follow building rules. Questions about a rule? Ask staff before it becomes an issue.

name: Noise & Disturbances
body: You and your neighbours are entitled to quiet enjoyment of your homes. Please keep noise down, especially {{quiet_hours}}, and ensure guests do the same. Repeated disturbances may lead to enforcement.

name: Absent From Unit Policy
body: Planning to be away from your unit for an extended period? You must notify {{org}} in writing. Your unit must remain your principal residence to keep your tenancy and subsidy. Questions: {{office_phone}}.

name: Internal Transfer Process
body: Interested in transferring to another unit? Transfers are based on eligibility and availability. Speak to your Housing Worker at {{office_phone}} to learn the criteria and join the transfer list.

name: Move-Out Process
body: Moving out? Give {{org}} 60 days written notice. Clean your unit, report damage, and return ALL keys/fobs to the office. You are financially responsible for removal of belongings left behind and for any damage you caused.

name: Annual Unit Inspections
body: Annual unit inspections at {{building}} begin {{date}}. Staff will check smoke/CO detectors, plumbing, and the general condition of your unit. We'll provide notice of your inspection date and time.

name: Unit Inspection — What to Expect
body: During your inspection, staff will: walk through the unit, test smoke/CO detectors, check plumbing, and take photos for our records. Please clear access to the kitchen sink and fan-coil area beforehand.

name: LTB Forms & Information
body: Need a Landlord and Tenant Board (LTB) form or information about your rights? Forms are at tribunalsontario.ca/ltb. Staff can point you to the right form — ask at the office.

name: Tenant Insurance Reminder
body: {{org}} insures the building, not your belongings. We strongly recommend tenant (contents) insurance to protect against fire, theft and water damage. Many policies cost just a few dollars a month.

name: Welcome to Your New Home
body: Welcome to {{org}}! Your building office is open {{office_hours}} at {{office_phone}}. Submit repairs by phone or email, pay rent on the 1st, and watch the lobby display for news. We're glad you're here.

name: Booking Amenity Space
body: Want to book the community/amenity room at {{building}}? Reservations are first-come, first-served and may require a deposit. Contact the office at {{office_phone}} to check availability and rules.

name: Laundry Room Hours
body: Laundry room hours at {{building}}: {{laundry_hours}}. Please be courteous — remove your laundry promptly and report any machine issues to {{office_phone}}.

name: Community Programs
body: {{org}} offers free community programs — services, employment help, and childcare. Get involved and connect with your neighbours. Details: {{office_phone}} or ask staff.

name: Significant Dates & Holidays
body: Dates to remember this month at {{building}}: {{dates}}. Office holiday hours: {{holiday_hours}}. Wishing you and your family well!

name: Annual Tenant Survey
body: Your voice matters! Complete the {{org}} Annual Tenant Survey by {{date}} and help us improve your building and services. Scan the QR code on the lobby display or ask staff for a paper copy.

name: Building Staff Contact Info
body: Your building team at {{building}}: Housing Manager {{manager}}, Maintenance Supervisor {{maint_super}}, Housing Worker {{worker}}. Office hours: {{office_hours}}. Phone: {{office_phone}}.

name: Building Improvements / Capital Plan
body: Coming to {{building}} this year: {{projects}}. These improvements enhance safety, comfort and energy efficiency. We'll post timelines and any disruptions in advance. Thank you for your patience.

name: RentSafeTO Information
body: {{building}} participates in the City of Toronto RentSafeTO program. You can view our most recent building evaluation and standards info, and contact the RentSafeTO team by calling 311.

name: Accessibility Equipment
body: Accessibility features at {{building}} include {{features}} (ramps, door buttons, railings). If you need an accommodation, contact the office at {{office_phone}} — we're here to help.

name: Request Translated Materials
body: Need this information in another language? Scan the QR code on the display or contact the office at {{office_phone}} to request translated materials.

name: RentCafe Tenant Portal — Coming Soon
body: Coming soon to {{org}}: the RentCafe Tenant Portal! Submit and track work orders, pay rent online, and upload documents — all from your phone. Watch for your launch details.

name: Telephone Triage System — Now Live
body: {{org}}'s new telephone system is live! Call {{office_phone}} for streamlined service: faster routing to housing staff, follow-up on your requests, and timely support.
